creator: 000.fbsd@quip.cz
text:
This would be a reversion to the original deprecated behavior, which causes different ports that depend on ImageMagick to request different versions of IM and then not install together because they have conflicting requests to ImageMagick. This is why this setting was deprecated for pecl-imagick, exactly as described by USES=magick entry
https://lists.freebsd.org/archives/freebsd-ports/2023-April/003785.html

quoting:

* For port maintainers:
-----------------------

Please check your ports with USES=magick in these cases:

* You have some X11 or NOX11 option for your port. If this option is
  *only* used to select an ImageMagick flavor, then please remove it, it
  isn't needed any more, because users can just use DEFAULT_VERSIONS as
  described above.

* You depend on a specific version and/or flavor, e.g.
  USES=   magick:6,nox11
  Then please check whether this is really needed! If possible, remove
  these options, so the dependency always uses the user-specified
  version/flavor. This is to be preferred because any ImageMagick*
  packages conflicts on install with any other.


In short - if a user sets DEFAULT_VERSIONS+= imagemagick=7-nox11
then all ports that require IM will be built with the same version of IM and there is no risk of conflicting IM vs IM-nox11 dependencies.
DEFAULT_VERSIONS is the way to go. 
DEFAULT_VERSIONS is the standard FreeBSD style.
